$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for v4's CharactersRepository ARRAY / sub-array
ops (Phase-2, the store-backed capstone sub-unit 4b). One committed file drives
BOTH sides:
- the v4 oracle (build-characters-arrays-fixture.ts + characters-arrays.ts)
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness characters_arrays tier-2 test).
WHAT IT PORTS: the systemPrompts / scenarios / partnerLinks mutators + the
favorite / controlled-by / Carina setters. Each sub-array op is the three-beat
shape findById (read overlay) -> mutate-in-memory -> update (write overlay):
the prompt/scenario ops reproject the Prompts/ / Scenarios/ vault folders; the
partner ops + setters write slim columns. The minted item id/timestamps never
reach disk (the projection writes <sanitize(name|title)>.md with content from
buildSystemPromptFile / buildScenarioFile; the read side re-derives the id from
the path), so the DB effect is deterministic.
FIXTURE: built by running v4's REAL repos.characters.create(character) so the
fixture holds a fully-formed character + vault, with ONE baked systemPrompt
(Default), ONE baked scenario (The Dim Tavern), and ONE baked partnerLink. Both
the oracle and the Rust port operate on a COPY of this SAME baked fixture, so
the minted ids/timestamps match. The character id is read back from the fixture
(SELECT id FROM characters) — both sides see the same one.
NAME RESOLUTION: the prompt/scenario id-taking ops (updateSystemPrompt,
setDefaultSystemPrompt, deleteSystemPrompt, updateScenario, removeScenario)
carry a targetName / targetTitle instead of an id; each side resolves it to the
current item's id via findById right before the op (exactly as a UI caller
would). The item id is path-derived (stableUuidFromString) so both sides agree;
the op effect is filename-based regardless.
OPS (Prompts/ state tracked in parens):
baked: Default.md(default).
1. addSystemPrompt Combat,default     -> Default demoted, Combat default.
2. addSystemPrompt Sleuth,not-default -> no demotion (count>0).
3. updateSystemPrompt Combat->Tactician (rename+content) -> sweep Combat.md.
4. setDefaultSystemPrompt Sleuth      -> Sleuth sole default.
5. deleteSystemPrompt Sleuth (default)-> sweep Sleuth.md, promote survivor[0].
scenarios (baked: The Dim Tavern.md):
6. addScenario Rooftop Chase.
7. updateScenario The Dim Tavern (content).
8. removeScenario Rooftop Chase       -> sweep.
partnerLinks (baked: one link, default):
9. addPartnerLink (new)               -> slim _update.
10. removePartnerLink (baked one)      -> slim _update.
setters:
11. setFavorite false   12. setControlledBy user   13. setCanBeCarina true.
REINDEX: the oracle drives the REAL ops (no QUILLTAP_JOB_CHILD), so
reindexSingleFile runs; the differential pins the link chunkCount and excludes
doc_mount_chunks (as for create/update).
NORMALIZATION: minted-values remap with ONE shared id-map across all six tables
(characters -> points -> folders -> files -> documents -> links, natural-key
order). FKs verify by relationship; timestamps -> <ts>; chunkCount -> <cc>. The
slim DB changes (isFavorite/controlledBy/canBeCarina/partnerLinks) and every
vault file's content diff EXACTLY.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key (the same one folders/tags/
wardrobe use). NOT the real Friday pepper; safe to commit.

character.name: Evangeline
character.identity: A wandering scholar of forgotten tongues.
character.title: the scholar

systemPrompts.name: Default
systemPrompts.content: You are Evangeline, a scholar of forgotten tongues.

scenarios.title: The Dim Tavern
scenarios.content: A dim tavern at dusk; rain against the panes.

ops.title: Rooftop Chase
ops.content: Across slick tiles under a bruised sky.

data.content: Now with thunder rattling the casements.
